channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Does MOUSE: P.I. For Hire Have Early Access? MOUSE: P.I. For Hire Demo Status, Explained
item.content:encoded:
The cartoonish aesthetic might be tempting you into MOUSE: P.I. For Hire, or maybe it’s just the outlandish violence. Either way, if you want to know if MOUSE: P.I. For Hire has early access or a demo to roll the movie ASAP, we’ve got you covered.

Insider Gaming has labeled MOUSE: P.I. For Hire as one of the biggest shooters of 2026. This means we’re excited for Jack Pepper to stomp on criminals, and you might feel the same. This is why you need to know about getting the game earlier in any way.

Can You Play MOUSE: P.I. For Hire Early Access?

There is no form of MOUSE: P.I. For Hire early access, which means you have to wait until launch day for the full game.

MOUSE: P.I. For Hire unleashed its different editions close to launch, showing off all the potential content for the game. This includes the standard base edition and the Mouseberg Edition, featuring Trading Cards, Post Cards, and more.

  • More Insider Gaming: Is MOUSE: PI For Hire On Xbox Game Pass?

One thing most definitely not included in any version of the game is early access. So, you can’t pay extra for the privilege to play 24, 48, or even 72 hours ahead of the official release date.

Does MOUSE: P.I. For Hire Have A Demo?

There is no official MOUSE: P.I. For Hire demo to download on any storefront, and it’s unclear if Fumi Games will release one later on.

The days of demo discs are so far behind us now, and even free demos on digital stores aren’t happening as much nowadays. It’s definitely a ‘boomer’ view, but it’s a sad sign of the times. Everyone can see gameplay footage and previews online everywhere in the modern era, and they’re essentially demos, but not really, as you don’t get to try it yourself.

  • More Insider Gaming: MOUSE: PI For Hire Release Date & Start Times

Insider Gaming did get to play an exclusive press demo for MOUSE, but that covers the extent of the game’s demo status. We’ll have to wait and see if the shooter gets a public demo, and if it does, check back for new updates, as we’ll let you know!

It’s sad that there’s no early access, in some ways, but good in others. It’s fun when everyone gets to play something at the same time.

Please check out Insider Gaming’s special MOUSE: P.I. For Hire Preview for a detailed look at the game, if you can’t wait.

MOBILE APP

https://insider-gaming.com/mouse-pi-for-hire-have-early-access-demo/

item.title: AAA Game Development Costs Are Now $300 Million or More, Says Insider
item.content:encoded:
A video game industry insider has revealed that AAA game development costs have reached $300 million and could exceed that amount.

Game development costs are increasing steadily, and many studios have had to resort to restructuring to remain viable amid this surge. Now, the latest numbers reveal that the average development cost has reached what GTA 5 cost when it was released, and those numbers were record-breaking then.

AAA Game Development Costs Are Skyrocketing

A new post on Bluesky by industry insider Jason Schreier reveals how much it costs to develop a AAA game nowadays. Schreier states it would be easier if studios posted their exact budgets; however, the numbers he has heard floating around are “$300 million or more — sometimes much more!”

Schrier clarified that these are the costs for studios based in the United States or Canada; games that cost less to develop were probably made “probably somewhere else”. He also explained that the numbers shared are based on “salaries + overhead and have nothing to do with executive compensation (which is mostly stock).”

In a report earlier this year, the insider also explained the various reasons for rising costs, with factors like labor, time to develop, mismanagement, the need for better graphics, and more coming into play.

In other news, an analyst has predicted that CD Projekt Red could spend close to $800 million on the development and marketing of The Witcher 4, a figure that matches the numbers shared recently. Additionally, a recent claim suggested that ARC Raiders cost around $75 million to make.

What are your thoughts on AAA game development costs hitting $300 million and sometimes surpassing it? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Jason Bundell commenting on PlayStation’s closure of Dark Outlaw Games. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

item.title: Is Super Meat Boy 3D on Xbox Game Pass?
item.content:encoded:
Super Meat Boy is back on the menu with the release of Super Meat Boy 3D, inevitably leading to questions about the title’s Xbox Game Pass availability.

Super Meat Boy 3D follows on from the original title and 2020’s Super Meat Boy Forever, but with a big twist that puts the platformer into the three-dimensional format for the first time.

If you’re wondering whether the title is available through Xbox Game Pass, or if you’ll need to splash out to secure a copy, Insider Gaming has you covered.

Can You Play Super Meat Boy 3D on Xbox Game Pass?

Yes, Super Meat Boy 3D is available to play with an Xbox Game Pass subscription, with the game’s inclusion in the service confirmed last year.

After the reveal during the Xbox Game Showcase in June 2025, a post on the Xbox Wire confirmed that Super Meat Boy 3D will be available at launch with an Xbox Game Pass subscription.

  • More Insider Gaming: All Super Meat Boy 3D Platforms, Explained

At this stage, it’s unclear whether Super Meat Boy 3D is a permanent addition to the Game Pass catalogue, or whether it will only be available for a limited time.

However, the Game Pass model is not as simple as it once was, with varying tiers of membership, and new titles like Super Meat Boy 3D are not available to every tier day one.

What Game Pass Subscription Tier is Needed For Super Meat Boy 3D?

A Game Pass Ultimate subscription is expected to be required to play Super Meat Boy 3D at launch on the platform, though this is yet to be officially confirmed.

Following changes to the Game Pass tier structure, an Ultimate subscription is required to obtain day one releases on the service, which is expected to remain the case for Super Meat Boy 3D. However, the Game Pass Premium tier adds new games within 12 months of their launch.

  • More Insider Gaming: Fortnite Teases Crossover With Super Meat Boy 3D

While that provides a potential option later down the line, Ultimate is expected to be the required subscription level to play the title immediately at launch.

For those with a more basic level of Xbox Game Pass, you’ll have to splash out separately if you want to play Super Meat Boy 3D anytime soon.

Are you planning to play Super Meat Boy 3D at launch? Share your plans in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For your next read, take a look at the full Life is Strange: Reunion trophy list, and find out Mouse: PI For Hire’s Game Pass status.

MOBILE APP

item.title: NYT Mini Crossword Hints Today—Answers For Thursday, March 26
item.content:encoded:
The NYT Mini Crossword is a popular, quick-to-complete word game to test yourself daily, but some of the puzzles can be difficult to complete, so here are some hints.

If you’ve found yourself stuck on today’s NYT Mini, Insider Gaming has some helpful hints and all the answers for the NYT Mini Crossword on Thursday, March 26, 2026.

IMPORTANT: The following information includes spoilers on today’s edition of the NYT Mini Crossword. If you don’t want the answer given to you, stop scrolling.

How to Play NYT Mini Crossword

The NYT Mini Crossword is available to play on the New York Times Games app or website, but, as of September 2025, a New York Times Game subscription is required.

The NYT Games Subscription is priced at $6.95 per month, but there are introductory offers available for other NYT subscriptions that include games access, which might be worth considering.

Without a Games subscription, access to the NYT Mini Crossword is blocked.

NYT Mini Crossword Clues (March 26)

Here are all the clues provided for the NYT Mini Crossword for March 26, 2026.

Across

1 – Degrees for boardroom execs

5 – “___ want for Christmas…”

6 – What Hamlet holds while giving his “Alas, poor Yorick!” speech

7 – Wild, as an animal

8 – Sphere

Down

1 – Word after ‘match’ or ‘mischief’

2 – Bit of writing on a book jacket

3 – Penne ___ vodka

4 – Window ledge

6 – Bay Area airport, for short

Additional Clues For NYT Mini (March 26)

Here are some additional clues to help you solve the NYT Mini Crossword on March 26, 2026.

Across

1 – 4 letter anagram of ‘BAMS’

5 – “___ ever wanted was you”

6 – Hard object inside your head

7 – Like a homeless cat

8 – Type of spider, ___ weaver

Down

1 – “Prepare to meet your ___”

2 – Short description for promotional purposes

3 – “Made with” in Italian cooking

4 – Similar to ledge, bracket

6 – Airport in San Francisco

All NYT Mini Crossword Answers (March 26)

Here are all the answers for the NYT Mini Crossword on March 26, 2026.

Across

1 – MBAS

5 – ALLI

6 – SKULL

7 – FERAL

8 – ORB

Down

1 – MAKER

2 – BLURB

3 – ALLA

4 – SILL

6 – SFO

When is The Next Game?

The NYT Mini Crossword refreshes daily at 10 PM ET on weekdays and Saturdays, and at 6 PM ET on Sundays.

As such, the next NYT Mini Crossword will be available at 7 PM PT/9 PM CT/10 PM ET/3 AM GMT/2 PM AET.

What is The NYT Mini Crossword?

The NYT Mini Crossword was introduced in 2014 as a shorter and quicker-to-complete game to accompany the main New York Times Crossword, which was initially released in 1942.

Until September 2025, the NYT Mini Crossword was free, but it has now been consolidated into a monthly NYT Games Subscription alongside several other popular games.

Did you struggle with the NYT Mini Crossword today? Share your thoughts in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For more word game hints, take a look at today’s Contexto, and check out today’s Wordle.

MOBILE APP
